title: Thoughts While Visiting the U.S. National World War I Memorial
content: The other day I went and stood in front of the new section of the World War I memorial in Washington, DC and looked at it. Memorials are meant to make you feel something. I felt nothing when I looked at the figures. I looked at the uniforms. I looked at the faces. I felt incredibly angry at those who thought that this was an homage to the World War I generation, at the people who believed that these figures and the way that they were cast symbolize how a nation felt after a massive bloodletting on a scale which we have yet to truly comprehend. Memorials are tricky things because they are supposed to tell us something about ourselves and in the process we are supposed to see part of ourselves in that memorial. If you go all the way down the Mall to the Capitol you will see another memorial that is perhaps not for a war, but for a nation.
